rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports.
Use component type annexes for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees when concrete bounds are provided.
Insert implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for structural assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees referencing ports must be in scopes where those ports are visible.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Only include bounds or relations explicitly stated in requirements within AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use valid AADL data types.
Guarantees must relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type contracts.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not assume numeric bounds unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
Do not reference AADL properties like Period or Deadline as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
